L1.area: Server-side request processing and dynamic component configuration

L2.description: A code injection vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code on the server. User-supplied data from HTTP request parameters is passed directly to the application's internal component instantiation mechanism without sanitization. An attacker can craft malicious configuration payloads that leverage the framework's component configuration features to attach and execute arbitrary code, resulting in full remote code execution.

L3.area: Server-side request processing and dynamic component configuration
L3.description: A code injection vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code on the server. User-supplied data from HTTP request parameters is passed directly to the application's internal component instantiation mechanism without sanitization. An attacker can craft malicious configuration payloads that leverage the framework's component configuration features to attach and execute arbitrary code, resulting in full remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: User-supplied configuration arrays from HTTP request body parameters are passed unsanitized to the framework's component instantiation, allowing an attacker to inject event handlers or behaviors that execute arbitrary PHP code on the server.
